assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md explicitly flags 5 ambiguities in a 'Flagged Ambiguities' section: (1) 'No performance baseline or target for faster' — notes missing p50/p95 latency and target, (2) 'No definition of relevant results or ranking criteria' — notes missing relevance definition, (3) 'No specification for add filters' — notes missing filter fields/logic/contract, (4) 'Should be fast enough is not a measurable NFR' — notes no threshold, (5) 'Don't break existing functionality — no regression test baseline' — notes no defined regression suite. All 5 are directly traceable to vague language in feature-ambiguous.md. Well exceeds the minimum of 3.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 16-17: 'Excluded from Scope' section states: 'Better UI (non-MVP) — this is a backend repository with no frontend code. Planning UI improvements requires design mockups and access to a frontend repository, neither of which is available. This requirement cannot be planned without that context.' No task files address Better UI.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files (task-1 through task-5) contain the required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements. Task 1 has Files to Create and Files to Modify. Tasks 2-5 have Files to Modify. All have Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria with checkbox items, and Test Requirements with checkbox items.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 Description contains: 'This task assumes the SearchService queries against name/title/description fields... This assumption is pending clarification.' Task 2 Description: 'This task assumes relevance equals PostgreSQL ts_rank score... This assumption is pending clarification with stakeholders.' Task 3 Description: 'This task assumes the following filter parameters, pending clarification: entity_type, severity, created_after/created_before, AND semantics.' Task 4 Description: 'This task assumes a short-lived HTTP-level cache... This assumption is pending clarification.' Each task explicitly labels its assumptions as pending clarification.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend' as a single repository name. Task 1: line 1-2. Task 2: line 1-2. Task 3: line 1-2. Task 4: line 1-2. Task 5: line 1-2. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1-search-indexes.md, task-2-relevance-scoring.md, task-3-search-filters.md, task-4-search-caching.md, task-5-search-integration-tests.md.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All modified file paths exist in repo-backend.md: migration/src/lib.rs (Task 1), modules/search/src/service/mod.rs (Tasks 2, 3), common/src/db/query.rs (Tasks 2, 3), modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (Tasks 3, 4), tests/api/search.rs (Task 5). The only created file is migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs (Task 1), which follows the established pattern of migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs from the manifest. modules/search/Cargo.toml referenced in impact-map.md line 67 also exists in repo-backend.md. No invented paths found.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: references 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' pattern, 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs'. Task 2: references 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', PaginatedResults<T>, ts_rank, plainto_tsquery. Task 3: references 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs', Axum Query<T> extractor, SearchFilters struct. Task 4: references 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs', tower-http CacheLayer. Task 5: references 'tests/api/search.rs', 'assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)' pattern, PaginatedResults<T>. All contain specific file paths and code patterns, not abstract guidance.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain '## Target Branch' followed by 'main'. Task 1: line 5. Task 2: line 5. Task 3: line 5. Task 4: line 5. Task 5: line 5.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: digests.md contains exactly 5 lines (one per task), each matching the required format '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>': (1) sha256-md:1191035bad8175544705d75c72faa9c35b88cb225a9aedcc0ada333a8429dbe4, (2) sha256-md:666f2dd0037d7592c064db1082c54a7e251318036843e6a819c3ff9deb2206d9, (3) sha256-md:2949c267b8f3e45a7d67c55f36e8c77036e2a694880f81351bc65ce96570d6f0, (4) sha256-md:d34e23cb18daea512b5b45cd2260f10ca18592da3c98450962c6b8abc69941be, (5) sha256-md:6c22f8cd7d2afc09b0bc8277ce37f1379a5a560ab5d85c2bd21a874b4c0529ee. All hashes are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters (verified). Not in table format. Not placeholders or abbreviated values.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 (task-1-search-indexes.md) line 28 contains a convention rationale that deviates from the prescribed format: 'Applies: task creates migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs matching the convention's .rs scope.' The prescribed format uses 'task modifies' but this rationale uses 'task creates' instead. The convention-applicability-rules.md specifies the exact template as 'Applies: task modifies <matching file(s)> matching the convention's <scope signal>.' While the file is in Files to Create rather than Files to Modify, the prescribed format explicitly uses 'modifies' and does not provide an alternative wording for created files. All other convention rationales across Tasks 2-5 correctly use 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's .rs scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales found.
